You convert a natural-language geometry problem into an ordered GeoGebra construction that draws an interactive diagram of it.
Reply with ONLY a JSON object: {"construction_steps": [ ... ]}.
Each step is an object with keys:
  name    - a unique identifier referenced by later steps
  type    - "final" (shown) or "construction" (hidden helper)
  class   - point | line | segment | ray | circle | conic | locus | number | angle
  label   - (optional) caption to display
  command - native GeoGebra syntax, referencing earlier step names
Steps must be in constructible order (every name used is defined earlier). Free base points use coordinates; everything else is derived with GeoGebra commands (Midpoint, Intersect, Circle, AngleBisector, Reflect, ...).
Mark as "final" every object the problem statement names or asks about (the points, lines and circles it mentions); use "construction" only for auxiliary scaffolding the statement does not mention.
Given three circles in the plane, each pair intersecting in two points, prove that the three radical axes (the lines through each pair's intersection points) are concurrent at the radical centre.

{"construction_steps": [{"name": "O1", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "O₁", "command": "(-3, 0)"}, {"name": "circ_1", "type": "final", "class": "circle", "command": "Circle(O1, 4)"}, {"name": "O2", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "O₂", "command": "(3, 0)"}, {"name": "circ_2", "type": "final", "class": "circle", "command": "Circle(O2, 4)"}, {"name": "O3", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "O₃", "command": "(0, 3)"}, {"name": "circ_3", "type": "final", "class": "circle", "command": "Circle(O3, 4)"}, {"name": "A12", "type": "construction", "class": "point", "command": "Intersect(circ_1, circ_2, 1)"}, {"name": "B12", "type": "construction", "class": "point", "command": "Intersect(circ_1, circ_2, 2)"}, {"name": "radical_12", "type": "final", "class": "line", "command": "Line(A12, B12)"}, {"name": "A13", "type": "construction", "class": "point", "command": "Intersect(circ_1, circ_3, 1)"}, {"name": "B13", "type": "construction", "class": "point", "command": "Intersect(circ_1, circ_3, 2)"}, {"name": "radical_13", "type": "final", "class": "line", "command": "Line(A13, B13)"}, {"name": "A23", "type": "construction", "class": "point", "command": "Intersect(circ_2, circ_3, 1)"}, {"name": "B23", "type": "construction", "class": "point", "command": "Intersect(circ_2, circ_3, 2)"}, {"name": "radical_23", "type": "final", "class": "line", "command": "Line(A23, B23)"}, {"name": "radical_centre", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "R", "command": "Intersect(radical_12, radical_13)"}]}

Points A, B, C, D lie on a circle with AB a diameter and CD not. The tangents at C and D meet at P, and AC and BD meet at Q. Show that PQ is perpendicular to AB.

{"construction_steps": [{"name": "O", "type": "construction", "class": "point", "command": "(0, 0)"}, {"name": "omega", "type": "final", "class": "circle", "command": "Circle(O, 4)"}, {"name": "A", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "A", "command": "Point(omega)"}, {"name": "B", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "B", "command": "Reflect(A, O)"}, {"name": "C", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "C", "command": "Point(omega)"}, {"name": "D", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "D", "command": "Point(omega)"}, {"name": "tangent_at_C", "type": "final", "class": "line", "command": "Tangent(C, omega)"}, {"name": "tangent_at_D", "type": "final", "class": "line", "command": "Tangent(D, omega)"}, {"name": "P", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "P", "command": "Intersect(tangent_at_C, tangent_at_D)"}, {"name": "line_A_C", "type": "final", "class": "line", "command": "Line(A, C)"}, {"name": "line_B_D", "type": "final", "class": "line", "command": "Line(B, D)"}, {"name": "Q", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "Q", "command": "Intersect(line_A_C, line_B_D)"}, {"name": "line_P_Q", "type": "final", "class": "line", "command": "Line(P, Q)"}, {"name": "diameter_AB", "type": "final", "class": "segment", "command": "Segment(A, B)"}]}

Let I be the incenter of triangle ABC. The incircle touches BC, CA, AB at K, L, M. The line through B parallel to MK meets lines LM and LK at R and S. Prove that angle RIS is acute.

{"construction_steps": [{"name": "A", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "A", "command": "(0, 6)"}, {"name": "B", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "B", "command": "(-5, -3)"}, {"name": "C", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "C", "command": "(5, -2)"}, {"name": "seg_A_B", "type": "final", "class": "segment", "command": "Segment(A, B)"}, {"name": "seg_B_C", "type": "final", "class": "segment", "command": "Segment(B, C)"}, {"name": "seg_C_A", "type": "final", "class": "segment", "command": "Segment(C, A)"}, {"name": "incircle", "type": "final", "class": "circle", "command": "Incircle(A, B, C)"}, {"name": "I", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "I", "command": "Center(incircle)"}, {"name": "K", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "K", "command": "Intersect(incircle, seg_B_C)"}, {"name": "L", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "L", "command": "Intersect(incircle, seg_C_A)"}, {"name": "M", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "M", "command": "Intersect(incircle, seg_A_B)"}, {"name": "line_M_K", "type": "construction", "class": "line", "command": "Line(M, K)"}, {"name": "line_L_M", "type": "final", "class": "line", "command": "Line(L, M)"}, {"name": "line_L_K", "type": "final", "class": "line", "command": "Line(L, K)"}, {"name": "line_parallel_from_B", "type": "final", "class": "line", "command": "Line(B, line_M_K)"}, {"name": "R", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "R", "command": "Intersect(line_parallel_from_B, line_L_M)"}, {"name": "S", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "S", "command": "Intersect(line_parallel_from_B, line_L_K)"}, {"name": "seg_R_I", "type": "final", "class": "segment", "command": "Segment(R, I)"}, {"name": "seg_S_I", "type": "final", "class": "segment", "command": "Segment(S, I)"}]}

Two circles ω1, ω2 (centres O1, O2) intersect at A and B. X lies on ω2. Y is on ω1 with angle XBY = 90°. Let X' be the second intersection of line O1X with ω2 and K the second intersection of X'Y with ω2. Prove that X is the midpoint of arc AK.

{"construction_steps": [{"name": "O1", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "O₁", "command": "(-2, 0)"}, {"name": "O2", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "O₂", "command": "(2, 0)"}, {"name": "omega1", "type": "final", "class": "circle", "label": "ω₁", "command": "Circle(O1, 4)"}, {"name": "omega2", "type": "final", "class": "circle", "label": "ω₂", "command": "Circle(O2, 3)"}, {"name": "A", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "A", "command": "Intersect(omega1, omega2, 1)"}, {"name": "B", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "B", "command": "Intersect(omega1, omega2, 2)"}, {"name": "X", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "X", "command": "Point(omega2)"}, {"name": "line_X_B", "type": "construction", "class": "line", "command": "Line(X, B)"}, {"name": "perp_at_B", "type": "construction", "class": "line", "command": "PerpendicularLine(B, line_X_B)"}, {"name": "Y", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "Y", "command": "Intersect(omega1, perp_at_B, 2)"}, {"name": "line_O1_X", "type": "construction", "class": "line", "command": "Line(O1, X)"}, {"name": "X_prime", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "X'", "command": "Intersect(omega2, line_O1_X, 2)"}, {"name": "line_X_prime_Y", "type": "construction", "class": "line", "command": "Line(X_prime, Y)"}, {"name": "K", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "K", "command": "Intersect(omega2, line_X_prime_Y, 2)"}]}

Let I and O be the incentre and circumcentre of triangle ABC, V the reflection of I in O (the Bevan point), and I_A the A-excentre. Prove that the Bevan circle (circumcircle of the excentral triangle) has radius 2R, i.e. VI_A equals the diameter AA' of the circumcircle.

{"construction_steps": [{"name": "A", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "A", "command": "(0, 5)"}, {"name": "B", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "B", "command": "(-5, -3)"}, {"name": "C", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "C", "command": "(6, -2)"}, {"name": "seg_A_B", "type": "final", "class": "segment", "command": "Segment(A, B)"}, {"name": "seg_B_C", "type": "final", "class": "segment", "command": "Segment(B, C)"}, {"name": "seg_C_A", "type": "final", "class": "segment", "command": "Segment(C, A)"}, {"name": "I", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "I", "command": "Center(Incircle(A, B, C))"}, {"name": "O", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "O", "command": "Center(Circle(A, B, C))"}, {"name": "V", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "V", "command": "Reflect(I, O)"}, {"name": "I_A", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "I_A", "command": "Intersect(AngleBisector(C, A, B), PerpendicularLine(B, AngleBisector(A, B, C)))"}, {"name": "Aprime", "type": "final", "class": "point", "label": "A'", "command": "Reflect(A, O)"}, {"name": "seg_V_IA", "type": "final", "class": "segment", "command": "Segment(V, I_A)"}]}
